steps.title: Identify the city motive
steps.content: The passage says people are creatures of the sun who love light and life, and that this is why they crowd into towns and cities. It then contrasts daytime comfort in nature with nighttime loneliness and fear.

steps.title: Eliminate weaker purposes
steps.content: Choice A is wrong because sadness is tied to nighttime isolation, not crowded areas. Choice B is wrong because the passage does not warn readers to appreciate rural beauty. Choice C reverses the point by claiming people prefer hillsides to cities. The answer is D.
